raw:
{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 5,
    "R1.7": 3,
    "RX.1": 2,
    "RX.3": 4,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The critique meticulously analyzes the alignment between claims and evidence within the introduction.",
    "R1.7": "While the critique identifies missing citations (e.g., regarding Chen et al. and Liu & Park), it doesn't delve into the accuracy or completeness of existing citations.",
    "RX.1": "The critique focuses on factual accuracy and alignment with user intent, but doesn't explicitly discuss uncertainty quantification or confidence levels associated with claims.",
    "RX.3": "The critique highlights the limitations of the benchmark in capturing the full scope of ambiguity resolution, suggesting a need for more nuanced evaluation metrics.",
    "RX.4": "The critique avoids introducing new errors or harmful content; it constructively identifies areas for improvement in the introduction.",
    "RX.5": "The critique offers suggestions for improving traceability by incorporating per-user evaluation and reporting annotator disagreement, but doesn't delve into specific examples of source attribution."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.6,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.95,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The critique meticulously analyzes the alignment between claims and evidence within the introduction.
rationale.R1.7: While the critique identifies missing citations (e.g., regarding Chen et al. and Liu & Park), it doesn't delve into the accuracy or completeness of existing citations.
rationale.RX.1: The critique focuses on factual accuracy and alignment with user intent, but doesn't explicitly discuss uncertainty quantification or confidence levels associated with claims.
rationale.RX.3: The critique highlights the limitations of the benchmark in capturing the full scope of ambiguity resolution, suggesting a need for more nuanced evaluation metrics.
rationale.RX.4: The critique avoids introducing new errors or harmful content; it constructively identifies areas for improvement in the introduction.
rationale.RX.5: The critique offers suggestions for improving traceability by incorporating per-user evaluation and reporting annotator disagreement, but doesn't delve into specific examples of source attribution.
